assertions.description: PaymentRequest is exposed — Secure Payment Confirmation rides on top of the Payment Request API.
navigator.secure_payment_confirmation_availability (or a SecurePaymentConfirmation namespace) exposes an availability() method.
Calling the availability hook returns a Thenable (Promise) — the API is async by spec.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const fn=navigator.securePaymentConfirmationAvailability||navigator.secure_payment_confirmation_availability;if(typeof fn!=='function')return true;const p=fn.call(navigator);return p&&typeof p.then==='function';}catch(e){return true;}})()

assertions.description: PublicKeyCredential is exposed — SPC enrollment reuses WebAuthn credentials.
PaymentRequest can be constructed with the 'secure-payment-confirmation' method identifier without throwing at construction time.

Before this API, authors had to construct a PaymentRequest to test availability — the new availability() API makes that unnecessary. We probe that the method is on a stable global hook (not requiring a PaymentRequest instance).
